remember: Recall the definition of stress and strain, and their units.
List the types of elastic deformation (tensile, compressive, and shear) and their effects on a solid.
Identify the materials that exhibit elastic behavior and those that exhibit plastic behavior.
Explain the concept of Hooke's law and its limitations.
understand: Understand the concept of Young's modulus and its relationship with elastic deformation.
Explain the difference between tensile strength and compressive strength.
Describe the effects of temperature and pressure on the mechanical properties of solids.
Explain the concept of modulus of rigidity and its measurement.
apply: Calculate the stress and strain in a solid due to a given force or pressure.
Determine the Young's modulus of a material from a given stress-strain graph.
Predict the behavior of a solid under different types of loading (tensile, compressive, and shear).
Design a simple experiment to measure the Young's modulus of a material.
analyze: Analyze the data from a stress-strain graph to determine the material's properties.
Compare the mechanical properties of different materials and explain the reasons for their differences.
Evaluate the effects of defects on the mechanical properties of solids.
Design a system to measure the modulus of rigidity of a material.

ncert_objectives: Explain the concept of stress and strain.
Define Young's modulus and its importance.
Explain the difference between tensile strength and compressive strength.
Describe the effects of temperature and pressure on the mechanical properties of solids.
board_exam_objectives: To calculate the stress and strain in a solid due to a given force or pressure.
To determine the Young's modulus of a material from a given stress-strain graph.
To predict the behavior of a solid under different types of loading (tensile, compressive, and shear).
To explain the concept of modulus of rigidity and its measurement.
